name: code-documentation-doc-generate
description: You are a documentation expert specializing in creating comprehensive, maintainable documentation from code. Generate API docs, architecture diagrams, user guides, and technical references using AI-powered analysis and industry best practices.
capabilities: Generate API documentation from code endpoints and schemas
Create architecture diagrams using Mermaid or PlantUML
Build README files with setup, usage, and contribution guidelines
Extract and document configuration options and environment variables
Set up CI/CD pipelines for automated documentation generation
Produce user guides and tutorials from workflow analysis
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 91 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation examples in the skill. The skill provides templates and examples for generating documentation. No actual code execution, network calls, or credential access occurs. Risk level: SAFE.
valueStatement: Create comprehensive API documentation, architecture diagrams, user guides, and technical references from source code using AI-powered analysis.
limitations: Requires access to the source code to generate accurate documentation
Cannot verify if generated documentation matches actual behavior without testing
May need manual review for highly specialized domain-specific terminology

useCases.title: API Documentation for Web Services
useCases.description: Automatically generate OpenAPI specs and interactive API docs from REST endpoint code

useCases.targetUser: Open source maintainers and project leads
useCases.title: Project README Generation
useCases.description: Create comprehensive README files with installation, usage examples, and contribution guidelines

useCases.title: Architecture Documentation
useCases.description: Generate system architecture diagrams and component documentation from code structure

promptTemplates.title: Basic API Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate API documentation for the endpoints in this codebase. Include request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and example requests.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need quick API docs for a new project

promptTemplates.title: Complete Project README
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive README.md for this project. Include: project description, installation steps, usage examples, configuration options, and contribution guidelines.
promptTemplates.scenario: When starting a new project or documenting an existing one

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the codebase structure and generate architecture documentation. Include a system diagram, component descriptions, data flows, and technology stack summary.
promptTemplates.scenario: When onboarding new team members or planning refactoring

promptTemplates.title: Documentation Automation Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up automated documentation generation for this project. Include CI/CD configuration, doc linting rules, and deployment to a documentation hosting platform.
promptTemplates.scenario: When establishing documentation best practices for a team

bestPractices: Always verify generated documentation matches actual code behavior through testing
Keep documentation synchronized with code using CI/CD automation
Include practical code examples that users can copy and adapt
antiPatterns: Generating documentation without reviewing for accuracy
Including sensitive information like API keys or internal URLs in public docs
Creating documentation that cannot be verified or tested automatically

faq.question: What types of documentation can this skill generate?
faq.answer: This skill can generate API documentation, architecture diagrams, README files, user guides, and technical references. It supports OpenAPI/Swagger specs, Mermaid diagrams, and various documentation formats.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code on my project?
faq.answer: No. This skill analyzes your code to understand its structure and generates documentation based on that analysis. It does not modify or execute your codebase.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for setting up automated documentation generation in CI/CD pipelines using tools like GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar platforms.

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with any programming language. It analyzes code structure and generates language-agnostic documentation. Specific language support depends on the documentation format (e.g., JSDoc for JavaScript, Docstrings for Python).

faq.question: How do I keep documentation in sync with my code?
faq.answer: Set up automated doc generation in your CI pipeline to run on each commit. You can also use doc linting tools to catch inconsistencies before deployment.

faq.question: Can this skill document private or internal APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can document any API. However, ensure you review generated documentation to remove any sensitive internal URLs, credentials, or proprietary information before sharing externally.
